kind: MODEL-DERIVED from RELAB meteorite templates (resampled to the 16 Gaia bands under the documented 44 nm Gaussian bandpass approximation; edge bands 374/1034 nm dropped for matching). Distinct from measured quantities.
matcher: C2.5 shared-covariance Gaussian (per-group centroid + one pooled Ledoit-Wolf covariance -> Mahalanobis; uniform class priors so template count cannot win; temperature-scaled, isotonic-calibrated). Replaces the C2 RandomForest, which was a template-density catch-all (diag/C25_MATCHER_FINDING.md). self-CV bal_acc 0.508, ECE 0.198, T 2.0.
honesty_policy: The Gaia DR3 asteroid-reflectance core sits ~8x beyond the RELAB manifold (median Mahalanobis d2 ~189 vs RELAB q95 23.1); a rigid de-bias does not close it. Confident Gaia-only meteorite-group analogs are therefore NOT honestly supportable. analog_status is overwhelmingly 'unresolved'; the soft analog_distribution is an INDICATOR ONLY; 'ok' is emitted only inside the RELAB manifold (d2<=q95) and above conf threshold. Primary composition product is complex-level taxon_esox (C4). confident 'ok' fraction 0.08%.
oc_policy: H/L/LL merged into ordinary_chondrite; oc_subgroup always 'unresolved'
external_nir_path: MIGRATED (C2.6): match_external_spectrum uses the C2.5 per-group matcher + RELAB-manifold gate (c26_external); the RandomForest external path is retired for composition. NIR ground-truth measured 93.7% inside the RELAB NIR manifold (self-q95 d2 13.41), median d2 1.5 - domain-valid, unlike the ~5%-inside Gaia core. Confident NIR analogs: 5 (all HED). Apophis d2 0.54 inside -> indicative OC, unresolved. Follow-up: full-NIR-range (to 2.5um) matcher beyond the Gaia grid would raise NIR confidence.
